Steps.Message:
Great news - Our engineers have now restored power to all 1393 customers in the Maidstone area by repairing a faulted piece of equipment upon an overhead network pole.

Thank you for your patience while our team worked hard to resolve the issue safely. We apologise the outage lasted longer than anticipated and we appreciate your understanding.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Steps.Message:
Hello, Donny from the messaging team here. Thanks for your patience today, our field staff and control room engineers are working to get your power back on as quickly as they can.

Our records show that some properties affected by today's power cut may have had other unexpected interruptions recently. If you have, we're sorry for further disruption. All power cuts are fully investigated by our engineers but can sometimes happen multiple times before we can safely find the issue and fix it.

We know that not everyone affected by today's power cut will have had other power cuts recently, but we want you to know that if you have, our customer services team will try to update as many customers as possible about our investigations.

Steps.Message:
We're sorry your power has now been off for several hours and we're working as quickly as possible to get you back on.

We know you may be concerned about your phone battery running low, so here are some tips on how you can prolong battery life: Set your phone to power saving mode, reduce screen brightness, turn off WiFi, Bluetooth and GPS (if not in use), cut apps running in the background and if possible top up using a car charger or portable battery pack.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 11:26 - Thanks for bearing with us over today. Our team of cable jointers have now narrowed down the suspected section of faulted underground cabling using their specialist equipment.

Our digging team have begun excavations, and these can take some time depending on the surface material, and how much space is needed to allow our engineers to assess the fault and make their repairs. To do this safely, we have had to turn more power off in the area. 

Our estimated time frame of 14:00-15:00 doesn't need to change currently. We'll continue to keep you updated, but our team are always here if you have any questions.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Engineers are already on site in the Canterbury Road area to investigate an overhead network fault currently affecting 125 properties. Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off.

Using an average timescale for this type of issue we're looking to have you back on between 01:30 and 02:30, however we must stress that this time may change, depending on what our staff find. We'll continue to update you with all of our progress.

Sent by Lisa at 20:54.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage affects multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you expect.
